name: concept-evidence-use-code-snippet-not-line-number
tags: concept store, concepts.json, evidence, line numbers, drift, snippet
body:
When recording `evidence` entries in concept_store/concepts.json, prefer a stable code snippet (e.g. the literal `builder.add_edge("load_turn", "cache_check")` call) over a file:line pointer.

Why: line numbers drift whenever comments or unrelated code get added above the referenced line — the pointer silently goes stale even though the underlying fact is still true. A grep-able snippet stays correct regardless of surrounding edits.

How to apply: when adding or updating a concept's evidence field (during /deploy's concept audit, task-introspection's concept-store audit, or ad hoc updates), use `"<module path>: <exact code substring>"` instead of `"<module path>:<line>"`. Existing line-number evidence doesn't need a mass migration — fix opportunistically when touched.

concepts.json edits are documentation-only (no runtime effect) — they don't need to go through /deploy's dev→test→main pipeline urgently; fine to batch with the next code change.
